summary: The code analysis confirms that generate_auth_data.go line 125 uses math/rand seeded with time.Now().UnixNano(), introducing weak randomness into the authentication procedure, while line 259 correctly uses crypto/rand for RAND generation.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction demonstrates the presence of weak PRNG usage in security-sensitive code through code inspection and highlights the design flaw.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the UDM service, verified line 125 contains rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano())), and verified line 259 uses crypto/rand.Read(). The design flaw is confirmed.
verification_method: The verify.sh script inspects the source code to confirm the weak PRNG pattern exists alongside proper crypto/rand usage.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code at generate_auth_data.go:125 contains weak PRNG seed
Source code at generate_auth_data.go:259 uses crypto/rand correctly
Code analysis confirms design flaw: both patterns coexist in same procedure
Weak PRNG could affect other variables if math/rand is used elsewhere in the flow

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the free5gc/udm repository from GitHub, checks out v3.2.10 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container.
